title: hypericum elongatum var. elongatum
intro: Hypericum elongatum var. elongatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of hyperforin and hypericin. The health benefits of St. John's Wort include its potential to reduce symptoms of mild to moderate depression, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its ability to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in a carrier oil or vinegar.
